qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default-enabled AdminServer exposes unauthenticated system properties and operator-only diagnostics
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes ZKConfig logging sensitive client configuration values to the client logfile, while this finding is a server-side AdminServer HTTP endpoint that exposes JVM system properties because no authentication or authorization gate protects GET /commands/*.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's root cause. GHSA-crhr-qqj8-rpxc is a client-side ZKConfig information leak to the client logfile at INFO level, but the session's only qualifying finding is an unauthenticated server-side AdminServer disclosure over HTTP.
evidence: The advisory details attribute the issue to improper handling of configuration values in ZKConfig and say sensitive client configuration is exposed in the client's logfile.
The advisory is classified under CWE-532, which aligns with sensitive-information-in-logs rather than a missing authentication check on an HTTP management endpoint.

The only qualifying finding report and PoC evidence describe GET /commands/sysp on the embedded AdminServer returning JVM system properties over unauthenticated HTTP, which is a different component, trigger, and disclosure sink.
